# .claude/project-scope.md
# Generated by /team-brief discovery step. Update manually if project scope changes.
# Read by: /team-design (Step 1), /team-plan (Step 1), /team-build (optional worktree), /team-qa (routing), /team-tdd (domain rows).
# generated_at: 2026-04-11

domains:
  - agentic-systems   # primary — container-per-thread Claude Agent SDK orchestrator
  - software-engineering  # secondary — Node.js/TypeScript backend, IPC, SQLite, messaging channels
  - llm-engineering   # tertiary — Claude Agent SDK wiring, streaming, prompt caching, model selection

primary_language: typescript
framework: none        # single-process Node.js orchestrator, no web framework
test_framework: vitest

relevant_global_skills:
  - agentic-systems
  - software-engineering
  - llm-engineering

description: >
  NanoClaw is a personal Claude assistant. Single-process Node.js/TypeScript
  orchestrator (src/index.ts) that routes messages from channels
  (WhatsApp/Slack/Discord/Telegram/Gmail) into per-thread containers running
  the Claude Agent SDK (container/agent-runner/). Host ↔ container IPC over
  stdin streams. Per-thread sticky sessions in SQLite. Channels self-register
  as skills at startup. Container skills (container/skills/) are loaded by
  agent-runner at runtime for browser/status/formatting capabilities.

  Quality gates: `npm run build && npm test` before review, then review swarm
  tiered by change type (trivial → /simplify, normal → /claw-review-swarm +
  /simplify, subsystem → /best-practice-check + review swarm + /simplify).
  For container code changes, rebuild with `./container/build.sh`.

  Security surface: IPC is untrusted — every execSync path that touches
  agent-supplied values must use execFileSync with argument arrays. Path
  inputs need containment checks. Credentials live in OneCLI Agent Vault,
  never passed to containers directly. Container filesystem: /workspace/group
  and /workspace/thread writable; /workspace/ root read-only; UID 1001.
